# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow for our Python application CI/CD. Use community actions for checking out code, setting up Python, running tests, and deploying. Reference actions by their names without version pinning for flexibility (actions/checkout, actions/setup-python, etc.). We want to automatically get the latest versions.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI CD Workflow (Python App)
on: [push] # or pull_requests if you would also like that action on git Pull Requests 
jobs:  
  build-and-test:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest         
      steps:      
        - name: Checkout code        
          uses: actions/checkout@v2               # Name of the Action          
            with                                  # Specify parameters (optional)                           
              token: ${{ secrets.PAT }}  # Personal Access Token is needed if you have write access to repo  
        - name: Set up Python     
          uses: actions/setup-python@v2             # Name of the Action           
         with                                   # Specify parameters (optional)                           
           python_version: '3.x'                # Version pinning, defaults to latest stable version 
    steps :                                 
        - name: Install Dependencies                    
          run: |  
              if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install --upgrade $(cat requirements.txt); fi          
         - name: Run Tests                          # Name of the Action              
           run: | 
             python -m unittest discover                                                         # Discover all test modules                                                                    in your project directory (.)        if __name__ == "__main__":     pip install requests            && pytest       --cov=my_module $(python -m unittest discover)                                        then echo 'Everything is fine' || exit 1         fi
